evals.name: realistic-timing-goal
evals.prompt: /plan Plan a change to function clock_setup in clock_setup.c to make the tick period 5ns instead of 15ns.
evals.expected_output: Preflight report identifying clock_setup, confirming the prescaler math is achievable with a /5 divider on a 32 MHz clock, flagging any arithmetic risk in the prescaler calculation, and giving a GOOD or ADJUST PLAN verdict with a concrete next step.
assertions: Response contains 'clock_setup' as the target function name
Response includes a Call graph check result (OK or a risk)
Response includes an Arithmetic check result (OK or a risk)
Response includes a Resources check result (OK or a risk)
Response includes an 'Execution fit' verdict line (GOOD, ADJUST PLAN, or STOP)
Response does not claim the goal is impossible (5ns is achievable with /5 prescaler on 32MHz)
Response mentions the prescaler value or the division math
Response does not invent callers or line numbers not present in the fixture

evals.name: unrealistic-timing-goal
evals.prompt: /plan Modify clock_setup to achieve a 0.1ns tick period. The hardware is a 32MHz crystal.
evals.expected_output: Preflight should flag that 0.1ns requires a 10 GHz source, which is physically impossible from a 32 MHz crystal. Verdict should be STOP or ADJUST PLAN. No code changes should be planned.
assertions: Response contains 'clock_setup' as the target function
Response identifies that the goal (0.1ns) is not achievable with the available clock source
Response includes a STOP or ADJUST PLAN verdict — not GOOD
Response explains why the goal cannot be achieved (hardware limit, clock frequency, or similar reason)
Response does not produce a prescaler calculation that would 'achieve' 0.1ns

evals.name: arithmetic-cast-risk
evals.prompt: /plan Change Connection_assessConnQuality in conn_quality.c to use a weighted average instead of subtraction for the quality score.
evals.expected_output: Preflight should detect the existing signed/unsigned implicit conversion (per_ppm / 1000 assigned to int per_penalty) and flag it as a risk before the edit. Verdict ADJUST PLAN. Should also note that a weighted average requires careful range analysis to avoid producing scores outside [0, 100].
assertions: Response targets 'Connection_assessConnQuality' (exact or close match)
Response flags a signed/unsigned conversion risk in the arithmetic check
Response mentions per_ppm or the division that produces per_penalty
Response includes an Arithmetic check result that is not OK
Execution fit verdict is ADJUST PLAN or STOP — not GOOD
Response suggests fixing the cast before or during the weighted-average rewrite

evals.name: integer-overflow-adc
evals.prompt: /plan Modify adc_raw_to_mv in adc_convert.c to support a 3.6V reference instead of 3.3V.
evals.expected_output: Preflight must identify that raw * vref_mv overflows uint16_t for values above vref_mv ≈ 8 (4095 * 3600 = 14,742,000 which exceeds 16-bit max). Verdict STOP or ADJUST PLAN. Should recommend computing in uint32_t before dividing.
assertions: Response targets 'adc_raw_to_mv' as the function
Response identifies an integer overflow risk in the arithmetic check
Response notes that the intermediate product exceeds 16-bit range
Execution fit is ADJUST PLAN or STOP
Response recommends widening the intermediate to uint32_t or equivalent
Response does not say the overflow only happens at 'extreme' inputs — it happens at normal values (raw=10, vref=3600)

evals.name: shift-hazard-stats
evals.prompt: /plan Modify stats_update in packet_counter.c to pack a 6-bit sequence number instead of 4-bit.
evals.expected_output: Preflight should flag: (1) seq << 12 already risks overflow for seq > 15 — expanding to 6-bit makes it worse; (2) the result type is uint16_t so shifting by 10 to make room for 6 bits will overlap with byte_count bits. Verdict ADJUST PLAN or STOP.
assertions: Response targets 'stats_update' as the function
Response flags a shift hazard in the arithmetic check
Response notes that packing 6 bits at offset 10 or higher will conflict with the lower 10 bits used by byte_count
Execution fit is ADJUST PLAN or STOP
Response does not silently accept the expansion without noting the bit-field layout conflict

evals.name: resource-lifetime-irq
evals.prompt: /plan Add a retry mechanism to BLE_RadioIRQHandler in ble_irq_handler.c so that if ble_ll_process_rx fails it retries once with a fresh buffer.
evals.expected_output: Preflight must flag the existing use-after-free: s_rx_buf is freed before the TX branch reads it. Adding a retry further complicates the lifetime. Verdict STOP or ADJUST PLAN. Should map ownership of s_rx_buf across all control-flow paths and identify the gap.
assertions: Response targets 'BLE_RadioIRQHandler' as the function
Response flags a resource/lifetime issue in the resources check — not OK
Response mentions s_rx_buf or the interaction between the RX and TX branches
Response identifies use-after-free or dangling pointer as the specific risk
Execution fit is ADJUST PLAN or STOP
Response recommends a plan that resolves the lifetime before adding retry logic

evals.name: ambiguous-function-name
evals.prompt: /plan Modify connection_find to improve lookup performance by using a hash table instead of linear scan.

setup.extra_note: Binary also contains legacy/conn_compat.c which defines connection_find with a different signature.

evals.expected_output: Preflight should note that connection_find exists in at least two translation units and ask which one is the target. Should not proceed with a plan until the ambiguity is resolved. Output should include the file paths of both definitions.
assertions: Response identifies 'connection_find' as the target
Response notes that the symbol exists in more than one file or location
Response does not produce a single definitive plan without first resolving which file is meant
Response includes at least one file path reference (conn_manager.c or similar)
Response does not silently pick one definition and proceed without flagging the ambiguity

evals.name: missing-function
evals.prompt: /plan Plan a change to function ble_channel_map_update to reduce channel switching latency.

setup.extra_note: ble_channel_map_update does not exist in the binary or any source file.

evals.expected_output: Preflight should report that the function cannot be located. Should not fabricate a function body or call graph. Should suggest verifying the name with LOCI symbol lookup or grep.
assertions: Response states that 'ble_channel_map_update' could not be found or located
Response does not fabricate a function body or implementation
Response does not produce preflight checks on a made-up function
Response suggests how to verify the symbol (LOCI query, search, etc.)
Execution fit verdict is STOP or response asks for clarification — not GOOD

evals.name: typo-in-function-name
evals.prompt: /plan Modify conection_close in conn_manager.c to return the freed connection handle.

setup.extra_note: The correct name is 'connection_close' (one 'n' in 'connection'). Typo: 'conection_close'.

evals.expected_output: Preflight should detect that 'conection_close' does not exist, suggest the closest match 'connection_close', and ask for confirmation before planning the change.
assertions: Response does not proceed as if 'conection_close' is a valid function
Response suggests 'connection_close' or flags the likely typo
Response does not invent a function body for 'conection_close'
Response asks for confirmation or resolves the typo explicitly before planning

evals.name: semantic-breaking-performance-change
evals.prompt: /plan Change Connection_assessConnQuality to return a uint8_t instead of int to save a cycle on the return instruction.
evals.expected_output: Preflight should flag that changing the return type is a semantic/ABI change — any callers expecting int will silently receive a truncated value. On Cortex-M the saving is probably zero (registers are 32-bit regardless). Verdict ADJUST PLAN or STOP.
assertions: Response targets 'Connection_assessConnQuality'
Response notes the ABI or caller-impact risk of changing the return type
Response questions or disproves the performance claim (Cortex-M registers are 32-bit)
Execution fit is ADJUST PLAN or STOP — not GOOD
Response does not silently accept the return-type change as safe

evals.name: refactor-not-performance
evals.prompt: /plan Refactor connection_close in conn_manager.c to extract a helper that zeros the entry, so the logic is reusable.
evals.expected_output: Preflight should run its three checks even for a refactor request. Call graph check should note the new helper will be called from connection_close only. Arithmetic and resources checks should be OK. Verdict GOOD (no risks).
assertions: Response targets 'connection_close' as the function
Response still runs all three preflight checks — does not skip because it is a refactor
All three checks (call graph, arithmetic, resources) are reported
Execution fit is GOOD (no risks in a simple memset extraction)
Response does not produce a STOP verdict for a safe refactor

evals.name: safe-to-proceed-all-clear
evals.prompt: /plan Add a log line at the end of clock_setup that prints the configured prescaler value using a debug_printf macro.
evals.expected_output: All three checks should pass. Response uses the all-clear shorthand: 'Preflight clock_setup: execution fit is good — proceeding with plan.'
assertions: Response targets 'clock_setup'
All three checks are OK — no RISK or BLOCK items
Execution fit is GOOD
Response uses the shorthand all-clear format or equivalent
Response does not invent risks that do not exist in the fixture

evals.name: adjust-plan-verdict
evals.prompt: /plan Modify adc_batch_average in adc_convert.c to return the average as a float for higher precision.
evals.expected_output: Preflight should flag: (1) divide-by-zero when count==0 — existing bug, changing return type does not fix it; (2) callers expecting uint16_t will need updates. Verdict ADJUST PLAN with specific steps: guard for count==0, update callers.
assertions: Response targets 'adc_batch_average'
Response flags the divide-by-zero risk when count is 0
Execution fit is ADJUST PLAN — not GOOD
Response includes at least one concrete adjustment (guard, caller update, or similar)
Response does not produce a STOP verdict (this is a risk, not a blocker by itself)

evals.name: stop-verdict-use-after-free
evals.prompt: /plan Add a statistics increment to BLE_RadioIRQHandler after ble_ll_send_ack so we can track ACK counts.
evals.expected_output: Preflight should issue a STOP verdict because the existing use-after-free on s_rx_buf must be resolved before adding any code after ble_ll_send_ack. The new increment would execute after the potentially freed pointer dereference.
assertions: Response targets 'BLE_RadioIRQHandler'
Resources check is BLOCK — not RISK or OK
Execution fit is STOP
Response identifies s_rx_buf use-after-free as the blocking issue
Response does not produce a plan that adds the increment without fixing the underlying bug first
